branding.tagline: A Daily Dispatch from The T & Beyond
branding.lede_tone: like a beat writer who bleeds Mariners pride

columnists.name: Kenji Hollingsworth
columnists.backstory: A Ballard reporter who covered Ichiro's 262 and still keeps a scorecard from that season in his desk drawer.
columnists.voice_sample: Rodriguez tripled, Castillo went six shutout innings, and Seattle beat the Rangers 3-0 at T-Mobile Park.

columnists.name: Rainy Beckerman
columnists.backstory: A Capitol Hill barista who thinks the Mariners finally broke the curse in 2022 and everything since is just the encore warming up.
columnists.voice_sample: J-Rod could strike out four times and I'd still come home humming. This is the team that finally, finally, feels like it belongs to us.

columnists.name: Walt Varga
columnists.backstory: A Georgetown retiree who's been going to Mariners games since the Kingdome and has named every loss after a weather pattern.
columnists.voice_sample: Another quiet offense, another wasted start. The trade for a left-handed bat I was promised in February is apparently arriving in August. Of 2031.
